steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole. The underlined sentence describes what other contemporaneous personal manuscripts contain, immediately before "whereas" signals a contrast with the HM. Ask: what job does this comparison sentence do for the rest of the passage?

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To provide context for the text's suggestion that the HM is an outlier among collections of its time.: The underlined portion establishes what typical manuscripts looked like (celebrated authors, elite readings), making the HM’s popular/practical focus stand out as unusual. This is exactly “providing context” for the HM’s outlier status. ✓
  • Choice B — To emphasize the ubiquity of hand-copied collections like the FA and the HM in medieval England.: The underlined portion says nothing about how common such collections were. Ubiquity is not discussed anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — To suggest that the FA is a poor point of comparison for a collection like the HM.: The opposite is true—the FA is used as a representative example precisely because it is a useful comparison. The underlined portion does not undermine the FA as a comparator. ✗
  • Choice D — To illustrate how the discussion of the HM earlier in the text can improve historians' understanding of the FA.: The passage never claims the HM improves understanding of the FA. The direction of insight runs from the comparison to the HM, not the other way around. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
